verses.text: They ask by what authority He does these things. Challenge. They question the right of the One sent from God.
They ask by what authority He does these things. Challenge. They question the right of the One sent from God.
He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or men? They reason among themselves. Cornered. His question exposes their insincerity.
He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or men? They reason among themselves. Cornered. His question exposes their insincerity.
He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or men? They reason among themselves. Cornered. His question exposes their insincerity.
Fearing the people, they say they cannot tell; so neither will He tell them. Evasion answered. Those who hide from truth forfeit further light.
Fearing the people, they say they cannot tell; so neither will He tell them. Evasion answered. Those who hide from truth forfeit further light.
Fearing the people, they say they cannot tell; so neither will He tell them. Evasion answered. Those who hide from truth forfeit further light.
A man lets out a vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by His own people.
A man lets out a vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by His own people.
A man lets out a vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by His own people.
A man lets out a vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by His own people.
He sends his beloved son, whom they slay; he will destroy them and give the vineyard to others. The heir killed. They reject the Son and earn ruin.
He sends his beloved son, whom they slay; he will destroy them and give the vineyard to others. The heir killed. They reject the Son and earn ruin.
He sends his beloved son, whom they slay; he will destroy them and give the vineyard to others. The heir killed. They reject the Son and earn ruin.
He sends his beloved son, whom they slay; he will destroy them and give the vineyard to others. The heir killed. They reject the Son and earn ruin.
'The stone which the builders rejected... is become the head of the corner'; it grinds to powder. Rejected yet exalted. Christ, refused, is made the cornerstone.
'The stone which the builders rejected... is become the head of the corner'; it grinds to powder. Rejected yet exalted. Christ, refused, is made the cornerstone.
Spies ask, Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? A baited trap. They hope to catch Him either way.
Spies ask, Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? A baited trap. They hope to catch Him either way.
Spies ask, Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? A baited trap. They hope to catch Him either way.
Spies ask, Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? A baited trap. They hope to catch Him either way.
Whose image? 'Render... unto Caesar... and unto God the things which be God's'; they marvel. Both due. We owe the state its tax and God ourselves.
Whose image? 'Render... unto Caesar... and unto God the things which be God's'; they marvel. Both due. We owe the state its tax and God ourselves.
Whose image? 'Render... unto Caesar... and unto God the things which be God's'; they marvel. Both due. We owe the state its tax and God ourselves.
Whose image? 'Render... unto Caesar... and unto God the things which be God's'; they marvel. Both due. We owe the state its tax and God ourselves.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They ridicule the life to come.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They ridicule the life to come.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They ridicule the life to come.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive the world beyond death.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive the world beyond death.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive the world beyond death.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive the world beyond death.
They that attain that world neither marry, but are 'equal unto the angels... the children of God.' Risen life. The resurrection world is far greater than this.
They that attain that world neither marry, but are 'equal unto the angels... the children of God.' Risen life. The resurrection world is far greater than this.
They that attain that world neither marry, but are 'equal unto the angels... the children of God.' Risen life. The resurrection world is far greater than this.
Moses called the Lord the God of Abraham; 'he is not a God of the dead, but of the living.' Proof of life. The patriarchs live still, for God is theirs.
Moses called the Lord the God of Abraham; 'he is not a God of the dead, but of the living.' Proof of life. The patriarchs live still, for God is theirs.
Some scribes approve, and none dare question Him more. Silenced. His wisdom puts every adversary to flight.
Some scribes approve, and none dare question Him more. Silenced. His wisdom puts every adversary to flight.
How is Christ David's son, when David calls Him Lord? Deeper truth. Messiah is both David's son and his divine Lord.
How is Christ David's son, when David calls Him Lord? Deeper truth. Messiah is both David's son and his divine Lord.
How is Christ David's son, when David calls Him Lord? Deeper truth. Messiah is both David's son and his divine Lord.
How is Christ David's son, when David calls Him Lord? Deeper truth. Messiah is both David's son and his divine Lord.
Beware the scribes who love robes and 'devour widows' houses'; they shall receive greater damnation. Hypocrisy judged. Religion as a show brings the heavier sentence.
Beware the scribes who love robes and 'devour widows' houses'; they shall receive greater damnation. Hypocrisy judged. Religion as a show brings the heavier sentence.
